title: sNucDrop-seq Protocol
author: Peng Hu
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.vfse3ne

steps.title: Prepare sucrose cushion (50 mL)
steps.instruction: Prepare sucrose cushion (50 mL): %s %s Cool down the ultracentrifuge to 4 °C before preparing buffers.

steps.title: Prepare barcoded beads (ChemGenes)
steps.instruction: Prepare barcoded beads (ChemGenes): Wash beads once with 30 mL of 100% ethanol and twice with 30 mL of TE-TW (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA and 0.01% Tween-20) 30 mL, 100% Ethanol 30 mL, TE-TW

steps.title: Remove the oil layer from the...
steps.instruction: Remove the oil layer from the bottom of the 50-mL tube.

steps.title: Prepare RT mix (makes 200 μL)
steps.instruction: Prepare RT mix (makes 200 μL): %s %s

steps.title: Prepare Exonuclease mix (makes 200 μL)
steps.instruction: Prepare Exonuclease mix (makes 200 μL): %s %s

steps.title: Wash beads once with 1 mL H 2 O
steps.instruction: Wash beads once with 1 mL H 2 O. Spin 1000X g for 1 min. 1 mL, H2O

steps.title: Preheat thermocycler to 55°C
steps.instruction: Preheat thermocycler to 55°C. For each sample, take out 550 pg of purified cDNA with H 2 O in a total volume of 5 μL to a PCR tube.

steps.title: Estimate the library concentration
steps.instruction: Estimate the library concentration: Conc. (nM) =

steps.title: Prepare homogenization buffer (50 mL)
steps.instruction: Prepare homogenization buffer (50 mL): %s %s ReagentsVol.H2O40.89 mL2 M Sucrose8 mL0.5 M CaCl2500 μL1 M Tris-HCl pH 8.0500 μL 3 M MgAc250 μL100% Triton50 μL0.5 M EDTA10 μL1 tablet protease inhibitor w/o EDTA

steps.title: Add 14 mL of sucrose cushion...
steps.instruction: Add 14 mL of sucrose cushion to the bottom of a 1 x 3.5 in (25 x 98 mm) centrifuge tube (Beckman). Keep centrifuge tube on ice. 14 mL, Sucrose cushion

steps.title: Add 12 mL of homogenization buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 12 mL of homogenization buffer into the dounce tissue grinder, douncing 21 times with loose pestle then 7 more times using tight pestle to release the nuclei from adult mouse cortical tissues. 12 mL, Homogenization buffer This step needs to be optimized for different brain regions and other adult tissues.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the homogenate atop the...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the homogenate atop the sucrose cushion in the centrifuge tube. Add an additional 10 mL of the homogenization buffer atop the homogenate. 12 mL, Homogenization buffer Add the solution into the centrifuge tube slowly.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 25,000 rpm for 2...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 25,000 rpm for 2 hours at 4 °C to pellet nuclei. 02:00:00, Centrifugation 4 °C

steps.title: After centrifugation, carefully remove the centrifuge...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, carefully remove the centrifuge tube from rotor and keep the tubes on ice. An off-white circular pellet containing nuclei should be visible at the bottom of centrifuge tube.

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant and add...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant and add 1 mL chilled resuspension buffer (0.01% BSA in DPBS with RNase inhibitor). Incubate on ice for 20 min beforere suspending the pellet. 1 mL, Resuspension buffer 00:20:00, Incubation on ice

steps.title: Resuspend nuclei pellet and transfer the suspension into a 1
steps.instruction: Resuspend nuclei pellet and transfer the suspension into a 1.5-mL lobind tube (Eppendorf). Wash centrifuge tube with 0.5 mL chilled resuspension buffer and add to Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Filter nuclei twice with 40-µm cell...
steps.instruction: Filter nuclei twice with 40-µm cell strainer and count the number of nuclei. Dilute nuclei to 100 nuclei/μL with resuspension buffer(0.01% BSA in DPBS with RNase inhibitor). Transfer 2 mL of nuclei suspension into a 3-mL Luer-lock syringe. The following steps were adapted from the Drop-seq protocol (Macosko et al., 2015).

steps.title: Pass beads through a 100-µm cell...
steps.instruction: Pass beads through a 100-µm cell strainer and count the number of beads.

steps.title: Re-suspend beads at 120 beads/μL concentration in 1
steps.instruction: Re-suspend beads at 120 beads/μL concentration in 1.5 mL lysis buffer for each sNucDrop-seq run. Transfer 1.5 mL of bead suspension into a 3-mL Luer lock syringe. 1.5 mL, Lysis Buffer

steps.title: Prepare lysis buffer (makes 1 mL)
steps.instruction: Prepare lysis buffer (makes 1 mL): %s %s ReagentsVol. (μL)H2O40020% Ficoll PM-40030020% Sarkosyl100.5 M EDTA401.0 M Tris-HCl, pH 7.52001.0 M DTT50

steps.title: Draw up 7 mL of droplet...
steps.instruction: Draw up 7 mL of droplet generation oil (Bio-Rad) into a 10-mL Luer-lock syringe. 7 mL, Droplet generation oil

steps.title: Connect 3 syringes (containing nuclei, beads...
steps.instruction: Connect 3 syringes (containing nuclei, beads and oil, respectively) to the Aquapel-coated PDMS Microfluidic device (µFluidix) with the following flow rate setting: %s %s Syringe ContentFlow Rate (μL/hr)Oil15,000Nuclei4,000Beads4,000

steps.title: Start the run by pressing “start”...
steps.instruction: Start the run by pressing “start” on the pumps in the following order: nuclei→ beads → oil.

steps.title: When the flow of droplets stabilizes,...
steps.instruction: When the flow of droplets stabilizes, collect ~20 μL of aqueous flow to examine the droplet quality. Check whether the droplet size is uniform and estimate the percentage of bead doublets (the doublet rate should be less than 5%).

steps.title: Once confirming the droplet quality, collect 1
steps.instruction: Once confirming the droplet quality, collect 1.0 mL of droplets into a 50-mL conical tube.

steps.title: Add 30 mL of room temperature...
steps.instruction: Add 30 mL of room temperature 6X SSC into the tube. 30 mL, 6X SSC

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Perfluorooctanol (PFO)...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Perfluorooctanol (PFO) into the tube in a fume hood. Shake, rigorously,the tube 4 times to break the droplets. Spin at 1000Xg for 1 min. 1 mL, PFO 00:01:00, Centrifugation

steps.title: Carefully remove the supernatant on top...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the supernatant on top and then add 20 mL of 6X SSC to to kick up the beads into solution. Wait a few seconds to allow the majority of the oil to sink to the bottom. Transfer the supernatant to a new 50-mL tube. 20 mL, 6X SSC

steps.title: Add 20 mL of 6X SSC...
steps.instruction: Add 20 mL of 6X SSC to kick up the beads into solution again. Transfer and combine the supernatant. 20 mL, 6X SSC

steps.title: Spin at 1000xg for 2 min to pellet the beads
steps.instruction: Spin at 1000xg for 2 min to pellet the beads. 00:02:00, Centrifugation Make the RT master mix.

steps.title: The beads are now pelleted to...
steps.instruction: The beads are now pelleted to the bottom of the tube. Carefully remove all but ~1 mL of liquid. Resuspend the beads with remaining liquid and transfer them to a 1.5-mL lobind tube.

steps.title: Spin 1000X g for 1min
steps.instruction: Spin 1000X g for 1min. Remove the supernatant. Wash beads twice with 1 mL of 6X SSC and then once with ~300 μL 2X RT buffer. Remove as much of the 2X RT buffer as you can without taking any beads. 00:01:00, Centrifugation 1 mL, 6X SSC 300 µL, 2X RT

steps.title: Add 200 μL of RT mix to the beads
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of RT mix to the beads. 200 µL, RT Mix

steps.title: Incubate beads at room temperature for...
steps.instruction: Incubate beads at room temperature for 30 min with rotation, then 150 min at 42 °Cwith rotation. 00:30:00, Room Temperature 02:30:00, at 42 °C

steps.title: Wash beads once with 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash beads once with 1 mL TE-SDS (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 1 mM EDTA and 0.5% SDS), twice with 1 mL TE-TW. 1 mL, TE-SDS 1 mL, TE-TW Beads can be stored at 4°C in TE-TW for at least one month.

steps.title: Wash beads once with 1 mL 10mM Tris-HCl pH 8
steps.instruction: Wash beads once with 1 mL 10mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, re-suspend in 200 μL of exonuclease mix. 1 mL, 10mM Tris-HCl pH8.0 200 µL, exonuclease mix

steps.title: Incubate beads at 37°C for 45 min with rotation
steps.instruction: Incubate beads at 37°C for 45 min with rotation. 00:45:00, Incubation at 37°C

steps.title: Wash beads once with 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash beads once with 1 mL TE-SDS, twice with 1 mL TE-TW. 1 mL, TE-SDS 1 mL, TE-TW

steps.title: Remove supernatant and re-suspend the beads...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and re-suspend the beads with 1 mL of H 2 O.Quickly transfer 1 μLof beads into a well of 96-well plate (containing 50 μL of H 2 O) and count the number of beads. Repeat bead counting three times and take the average. 1 mL, H2O The expected bead concentration is 40-70 beads/μL for each run (~1 mL of droplets).

steps.title: Transfer an aliquot of 6,000 beads...
steps.instruction: Transfer an aliquot of 6,000 beads (corresponding to ~100 nuclei) into a PCR tube. Spin down and remove the supernatant, then re-suspend the beads with 50 μL PCR mix: %s %s ReagentsVol. (μL)KAPA HiFi HS Readymix25100 μM TSO-PCR primer0.4H2O24.6 Store the remaining beads in TE-TW at 4°C. Run 1st round of TSO-PCR to determine the optimal number of PCR amplification cycle to avoid over-amplification.

steps.title: Run 1st round of TSO-PCR.PCR program
steps.instruction: Run 1st round of TSO-PCR.PCR program: 95 °C for 3 minutes (min) 4 cycles of: 98 °C for 20 seconds (s) 65 °C for 45 s 72°C for 3 min 9 cycles of: 98 °C for 20 s 67 °C for 20 s 72 °C for 3 min Then: 72 °C for 5 min 4 °C forever

steps.title: Purify PCR productstwice with0
steps.instruction: Purify PCR productstwice with0.6X (30 μL) SPRI beads. Elute in 10 μL H 2 O. 30 µL, SPRI beads 10 µL, H2O

steps.title: Measure the concentration of PCR products by Qubit
steps.instruction: Measure the concentration of PCR products by Qubit. For mouse cortical nuclei, the expected concentrationis 100-600 pg/μL.

steps.title: Perform real-time PCR analysis to determine...
steps.instruction: Perform real-time PCR analysis to determine the additional number of PCR cycles needed for optimal cDNA amplification. %s %s ReagentsVol. (μL)Purified cDNA125 μM TSO-PCR primer0.22X KAPA FAST qPCR Readymix5H2O3.8

steps.title: Run real-time PCR with the following program
steps.instruction: Run real-time PCR with the following program: 95 °C for 3 min 25 cycles of: 95 °C for 15 s 63 °C for 30 s 72°C for 30 s

steps.title: Quantify the cDNA library by Qubit...
steps.instruction: Quantify the cDNA library by Qubit (for mouse cortical nuclei, the expected concentrationis 800-2000 pg/μL) and run the bioanalyzer to check the average fragment size of the purified cDNA library (the expected average size of cDNA library is 1300-2000 bp).

steps.title: After determining the optimal PCR cycle...
steps.instruction: After determining the optimal PCR cycle number, perform large-scale TSO-PCR on remaining beads. Wash the remaining beads twice with 1 mL H 2 O. Apportion 6,000 beads for each PCR reaction. Spin down and remove the supernatant, then re-suspend the beads with 50 μL PCR mix. 1 mL, H2O 50 µL, PCR Mix PCR program: 95 °C for 3 min 4 cycles of: 98 °C for 20 s 65 °C for 45 s 72 °C for 3 min 9 plus additional cycles of: 98 °C for 20 s 67 °C for 20 s 72 °C for 3 min Then: 72 °C for 5 min 4 °C forever

steps.title: Combine the PCR product for a...
steps.instruction: Combine the PCR product for a given sample into a 1.5-mL lobind tube and purify twice with 0.6X SPRI beads. Elute the cDNA in H 2 O (in 1/5 the volume of the PCR product).

steps.title: Add 10 μL of Nextera TD...
steps.instruction: Add 10 μL of Nextera TD buffer and 5 μL of Amplicon Tagmentation enzyme to each reaction. Mix by pipetting ~5 times. 10 µL, Nextera

steps.title: Incubate at 55 °C for 5 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 55 °C for 5 min. 00:05:00, Incubation

steps.title: Add 5 μL of Neutralization Buffer to each reaction
steps.instruction: Add 5 μL of Neutralization Buffer to each reaction. Mix by pipetting ~5 times. Spin down and incubate at room temperature for 5 min. 5 µL, Neutralization Buffer 00:05:00, Incubation

steps.title: Add to each PCR tube in the following order
steps.instruction: Add to each PCR tube in the following order: %s %s ReagentsVol. (μL)Nextra PCR mix152 μM P5-TSO hybrid primer52 μM Nextera N70X oligo5

steps.title: PCR program
steps.instruction: PCR program: 95 °C for 30 s 12 cycles of: 95 °C for 10 s 55 °C for 30 s 72 °C for 30 s Then : 72 °C for 5 min 4 °C forever

steps.title: Purify PCR product twice with 0
steps.instruction: Purify PCR product twice with 0.6XSPRI beads. Elute the cDNA in 10μL H 2 O. 10 µL, H20

steps.title: Quantify the concentration of cDNA library...
steps.instruction: Quantify the concentration of cDNA library by Qubit and check the average fragment size of the purified cDNA library by Bioanalyzer (the expected fragment size is500-700 bp).

steps.title: For sequencing on Illumina NextSeq 500...
steps.instruction: For sequencing on Illumina NextSeq 500 (75-cycle High Output v2 Kit), denature the library and dilute to 2.0 pM. Load diluted library to position 10. Dilute custom Read1 primer (0.3 μM) and load it to position 7. For sequencing multiple libraries in one flowcell, use following sequencing specification: 20 bp (Read1; custom read1 primer), 8 bp (Index1), and 50-60 bp (Read2).

steps.title: Spin down nuclei at 5,000 rpm...
steps.instruction: Spin down nuclei at 5,000 rpm for 15 minutes at 4 °C to pellet nuclei. Remove supernatant, and resuspend in 1.5 mL resuspension buffer. 00:15:00, Centrifugation 1.5 mL, Resuspension buffer To remove any excess sucrose cushion from the buffer.
